title: 10 best herbal creams for stomach cramps

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with stomach cramps because they contain soothing compounds that calm the digestive system. The anti-inflammatory properties in chamomile ease muscle spasms, reducing discomfort and pain caused by cramps. Additionally, the cream's gentle warmth relaxes the abdominal muscles, allowing them to release tension and return to normal function. As a result, herbal chamomile creams provide fast and effective relief from stomach cramps, helping individuals find comfort and alleviation from digestive distress.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Chamomile contains apigenin, a flavonoid that has anti-inflammatory properties, which helps to reduce inflammation and relieve stomach cramp discomfort.
Antispasmodic effects: The essential oil in chamomile, bisabolol, has antispasmodic properties that help to relax muscle spasms and ease cramping in the stomach.

Soothing and calming effects: Chamomile's apigenin and flavonoids have a calming effect on the digestive system, helping to reduce anxiety and stress-related symptoms that can contribute to stomach cramps, such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS).
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which help to soothe and calm digestive issues associated with stomach cramps.
Flowers: Known for their anti-inflammatory properties, chamomile flowers can help reduce inflammation and ease cramp-related discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried chamomile flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground chamomile powder with sweet almond oil in a ratio of 10% chamomile to 90% oil.
Combine the chamomile-oil mixture with beeswax pellets and heat it gently over low heat for 30 minutes stirring occasionally.
Remove from heat and let the mixture cool until it reaches a consistency suitable for whipping cream; whip vigorously using an electric mixer.
Fill sterilized containers with the whipped cream and store in the refrigerator at 40°f to 45°f for up to 2 weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm creams helps with stomach cramps because of its natural calming and soothing properties. The herb, Melissa officinalis, contains rosmarinic acid, a compound that relaxes digestive muscles and reduces spasms, alleviating abdominal discomfort. When applied topically as a cream, lemon balm's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant agents help to ease stomach cramping, bloating, and nausea associated with menstrual cramps, IBS, and other digestive issues, promoting overall gut health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Lemon balm's antispasmodic properties help to relax the smooth muscle in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramping associated with stomach cramps.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds found in lemon balm cream may help reduce inflammation and irritation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of stomach cramps such as bloating and discomfort.

Anxiolytic properties: Lemon balm's anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) effects can also contribute to its ability to alleviate stomach cramps by reducing stress and promoting relaxation, which can help ease muscle tension and spasms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and volatile oils, roots are a good source of calming compounds that can help soothe stomach cramps.
Leaves: The leaves contain high amounts of rosmarinic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and alleviate abdominal pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves and rinse them gently in cold water.
Chop the leaves into small pieces and steep them in boiling water for 10 minutes.
Strain the liquid and combine it with coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture slowly over low heat until it reaches a temperature of 120 degrees fahrenheit.
Remove from heat and let cool to room temperature before transferring to containers.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with stomach cramps because they contain compounds like glycyrrhizin, which have anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the digestive system. The creams also contain other herbal ingredients like marshmallow and chamomile, which calm the gut lining and reduce inflammation caused by irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) or other gastrointestinal issues. As a result, herbal licorice creams provide quick relief from stomach cramps, bloating, and discomfort, allowing individuals to feel more comfortable and relaxed.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which helps to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of stomach cramps such as pain and discomfort.
Licoric acid: This flavonoid has antioxidant properties, which can help to protect the stomach lining from damage caused by stress, bacteria, or other factors that may contribute to stomach cramps.
Mucilage: The gel-like substance found in licorice root has a soothing and protective effect on the mucous membranes of the digestive tract, providing relief from stomach cramps and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Licorice roots are rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties and helps soothe stomach cramps.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the licorice plant contain isoflavones, which have antispasmodic effects, relaxing muscles and reducing cramp severity.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and grind them into a fine powder weighing exactly 20 grams per serving.
Combine the powdered root with 30 milliliters of sweet almond oil and 15 milliliters of beeswax in a heat-resistant mixing bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10 to 15 minutes or until the beeswax has fully melted and the mixture reaches a temperature of 150°f to 160°f.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool slightly before whipping it with an electric mixer for 2 to 3 minutes or until it thickens into a smooth cream consistency.
Transfer the cream to sterilized glass jars and store them in a cool, dry place for up to 6 months or refrigerate them for up to 2 years without refrigeration.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger creams helps with stomach cramps because they contain natural compounds that ease digestive discomfort. The active ingredients in ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, work to relax the muscles in the stomach, reducing spasms and cramping. Additionally, herbal ginger creams often include soothing herbs like peppermint and chamomile, which calm inflammation and alleviate nausea. By applying these creams topically, they quickly penetrate the skin, providing targeted relief from stomach cramps and promoting a sense of digestive harmony.
remedy_properties: Shogaol: Shogaol, a bioactive compound found in ginger, has potent anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, which can help to alleviate stomach cramps by reducing inflammation and relaxing muscle spasms.

Gingerols: Gingerols, another set of bioactive compounds found in ginger, have been shown to possess antispasmodic and analgesic properties, making them effective in relieving stomach cramps by decreasing muscle contractions and pain perception.

Volatile oils: The volatile oils present in herbal ginger creams, such as [6]-gingerol and [10]-gingerol, have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation that may be contributing to stomach cramps.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The thick, underground stems of the ginger plant contain compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have natural anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate stomach cramps.
Roots: Ginger roots are rich in volatile oils that have analgesic and antispasmodic properties, making them effective in relieving stomach cramps caused by digestive issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh ginger rhizomes and clean them thoroughly under running water.
Chop the ginger into small pieces and combine with 100ml of distilled water in a saucepan.
Boil the mixture for 15 minutes then strain it through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a bowl.
Mix the resulting ginger extract with 50g of beeswax and 20g of sweet almond oil in a double boiler.
Stir the mixture until the beeswax is fully melted then pour it into small glass containers for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cornflower creams helps with stomach cramps because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. Antispasmodic properties: The phenolic compounds present in cornflower cream, such as rosmarinic acid, can help relax smooth muscles in the digestive system, reducing spasms and cramping associated with stomach pain.

Soothing and protective properties: Cornflower's mucilages and saponins create a soothing film on the skin and mucous membranes, providing relief from irritations and protecting the digestive tract from further damage.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm digestive issues, making them ideal for relieving stomach cramp symptoms.
Fruits: The fruits contain flavonoids and terpenes that have antioxidant and analgesic properties, which can help reduce inflammation and ease abdominal pain associated with stomach cramps.
remedy_recipe: Gather one part dried centaurea cyanus flowers, two parts coconut oil, and three drops of lavender essential oil.
Combine the dried flowers and coconut oil in a heat-resistant glass bowl and let it steep for 4 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into another bowl, discarding the solids.
Add the lavender essential oil to the infused oil and mix well, then transfer the mixture to a clean glass jar.
Store the cream in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks, shaking well before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with stomach cramps because it contains calendula, a natural anti-inflammatory agent that soothes and calms the digestive tract. When applied topically to the abdominal area, the cream reduces inflammation and eases muscle spasms, providing relief from cramping and discomfort. Additionally, calendula has been shown to possess antimicrobial properties, which can help combat underlying infections that may be contributing to stomach cramps. Antispasmodic properties: The herbs in pot marigold cream, such as chamazulene, possess antispasmodic properties that help relax smooth muscle tissue in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramping associated with IBS and other gastrointestinal disorders.

Sothiol antioxidant effects: Pot marigold creams contain sulfurous compounds that exhibit sothiol antioxidant effects, which help neutralize free radicals and oxidative stress in the gut lining, reducing inflammation and discomfort caused by stomach cramps.

remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality calendula flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder within 2 hours of harvesting.
Combine the ground calendula with sweet almond oil at a ratio of 10% herb to 90% carrier oil in a clean glass jar for infusing.
Infuse the mixture under indirect sunlight or low heat for 4-6 weeks, shaking the jar daily, until the liquid turns golden yellow and fragrant.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids, and label it as "calendula infused oil".
Mix the calendula infused oil with beeswax and vitamin e oil in a double boiler at 160°f to 180°f for 30 minutes until the beeswax melts and the mixture thickens into a creamy consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel creams helps with stomach cramps because they contain essential oils that stimulate digestive enzymes and soothe irritated linings in the stomach. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh foeniculum vulgare leaves and flowers in the morning after dew has dried.
Combine one part of freshly harvested plant material with three parts of carrier oil like sweet almond or olive oil.
Steep the mixture for six hours in a double boiler or a warm place shielded from direct sunlight.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass jar discarding solids.
Add one teaspoon of beeswax and two tablespoons of coconut oil to the strained oil then stir until fully incorporated taking approximately 10 minutes.

remedy_recipe: Weigh out 100 grams of high-quality beeswax and 200 grams of sweet almond oil in a small saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat for approximately 30 minutes or until the beeswax is fully melted.
Add 20 milliliters of peppermint essential oil to the melted wax mixture and stir well to combine.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken for at least 15 minutes before whipping with a handheld electric mixer.
Pour the whipped cream into sterilized jars and seal immediately, allowing it to set at room temperature.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh turmeric rhizomes and peel them thoroughly to remove any impurities.

Grind dried turmeric into fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Mix 20g of powdered turmeric with 100ml of coconut oil and 50ml of beeswax in a heat-proof bowl.
Heat the mixture under low temperature for 30-40 minutes until the beeswax melts completely.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh sambucus nigra flowers and leaves in the early morning, selecting only those free of pests or diseases.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator on low heat for 6-8 hours or air dry for several days.
Combine 50g of dried sambucus nigra with 100ml of cold-pressed sweet almond oil and 20ml of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over medium heat, stirring frequently, until the beeswax is fully melted and the mixture reaches a temperature of 160°f.
Remove the cream from heat, allow it to cool to 120°f, then strain through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into sterilized glass jars for storage.

Combine one part dried crataegus monogyna with two parts coconut oil and two tablespoons of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over medium heat stirring occasionally until melted and well combined then allow to cool slightly.
Pour the warm cream into sterilized glass jars leaving 1/4 inch headspace and seal immediately for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe creams helps with stomach cramps because of its anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and irritation. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera leaves from your garden or store them in the refrigerator for later use.

Combine 20 grams of aloe vera gel with 10 grams of calendula flowers and 5 grams of chamomile flowers in a blender.
Blend the mixture on low speed for exactly 2 minutes to create a smooth paste taking approximately 5 minutes.
Mix the paste with 50 grams of coconut oil and 25 grams of beeswax in a double boiler heating it at 160 degrees fahrenheit for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Purple coneflower contains anti-inflammatory compounds like triterpenoid saponins, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate abdominal pain associated with stomach cramps.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves in the early morning or late afternoon when they are at peak potency and dry them immediately to prevent degradation.

Strain the infused liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass jar, discarding the solids and retaining the liquid which will be used as the herbal extract.

Mix 100g of beeswax with 50g of coconut oil and heat them gently in a double boiler until they are fully incorporated and melted, then stir in the echinacea purpurea herbal extract to create the cream base.
Pour the cooled cream base into sterilized glass jars or containers and store them in a cool dark place for at least 24 hours before use, allowing the ingredients to mature and blend together.

remedy_recipe: Gather dried tilia platyphyllos flowers and calendula petals in equal quantities weighing approximately 20g each.

Rhyzomes: Contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, making them effective in alleviating stomach cramp symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh rumex crispus leaves and stems, rinse them thoroughly with cool water.
Combine the herbs with 2 tablespoons of glycerin in a blender or food processor for 30 seconds.
Add 2 ounces of beeswax and 6 ounces of coconut oil to the mixture, blend until smooth.
Heat the mixture at 160°f for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, to create a homogeneous consistency.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass jar, let it cool completely.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Field horsetail creams helps with stomach cramps because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The cream contains saponins, a type of phytochemical that soothes irritated tissues and reduces inflammation in the digestive tract. When applied topically to the abdomen, it calms spasms and eases cramping pain caused by conditions like IBS, food poisoning, or menstrual cramps. Additionally, field horsetail's antimicrobial properties help eliminate any underlying infections that may be contributing to stomach discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Field Horsetail contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and saponins, which help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating stomach cramps caused by inflammation.

Antispasmodic properties: The herb has antispasmodic properties that can help relax smooth muscle contractions in the digestive system, thereby relieving spasms and cramps associated with irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), Crohn's disease, and other conditions.

Antioxidant activity: Field Horsetail's antioxidant properties can help protect the digestive tract from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which may contribute to stomach cramps and other gastrointestinal issues.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm stomach cramps by reducing inflammation and discomfort.
Bark: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids that help alleviate symptoms of stomach cramps, including bloating and digestive issues.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried equisetum arvense herb and grind it into fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 3 minutes.
Combine the ground equisetum arvense with 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a small saucepan over low heat stirring constantly for 10 minutes.
Remove the saucepan from heat and let the mixture steep for 30 minutes to allow the herbal properties to infuse into the oil.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bowl discarding the solids and allowing it to cool at room temperature for 2 hours.
Whip the cooled oil mixture with an electric mixer until it forms a smooth cream consistency before transferring it to airtight containers for storage and use within 6 weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St john's wort creams helps with stomach cramps because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe irritated tissues in the digestive tract. The cream's active ingredients, such as hyperforin and hypericin, work to reduce inflammation and relax muscle spasms, providing quick relief from cramping discomfort. By calming the gut and reducing discomfort, St john's wort creams can help alleviate stomach cramps associated with IBS, menstrual periods, and other digestive issues, promoting a sense of calm and comfort.
remedy_properties: Hyperforin: Hyperforin, a bioactive compound found in St John's Wort, has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, helping to reduce pain and discomfort associated with stomach cramps.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in St John's Wort creams have antioxidant properties that help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to stomach cramps.
Tannins: Tannins, a type of polyphenol, have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, helping to relax smooth muscle contractions in the digestive tract and alleviate stomach cramp symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help reduce inflammation and ease muscle spasms associated with stomach cramps.
Barks: Contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in soothing digestive discomforts.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hypericum perforatum leaves and flowers for immediate use or drying for later preparation.
Dry the hypericum leaves and flowers in a cool dark place for at least 24 hours to preserve their active compounds.
Combine one part dried hypericum leaf and flower mixture with three parts coconut oil and heat gently until infused.
Strain the infusion through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove the herbal solids and discard them.
Mix the remaining infused oil with beeswax in a 1:1 ratio and melt together at low heat for about 10 minutes.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for stomach cramps are topical ointments infused with natural herbs and botanicals, specifically designed to provide relief from abdominal discomfort and pain caused by stomach cramps. These creams work by applying a soothing and calming effect to the skin, reducing inflammation and relaxing muscle spasms. Examples of herbal creams that help with stomach cramps include peppermint oil-based creams, which can ease IBS symptoms, and chamomile cream, which calms digestive issues. By providing fast and effective relief, these creams improve daily lives by allowing individuals to manage their symptoms and maintain a healthy digestive system, reducing the need for medication and promoting overall well-being.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat stomach cramps with herbal creams is by using a combination of anti-inflammatory and soothing ingredients. Look for creams containing natural herbs like ginger, peppermint, and chamomile, which help reduce inflammation and relax the muscles in your abdomen. Apply the cream topically to the affected area and gently massage it in until absorbed. This can provide fast and effective relief from stomach cramps.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to stomach cramps that are treated with herbal creams are menstrual cramps, fibromyalgia, and arthritis. Herbal creams containing ingredients such as arnica, ginger, and capsaicin can help alleviate pain and reduce inflammation associated with these conditions. These natural remedies can provide relief without the side effects of over-the-counter medications, making them a popular alternative for many individuals seeking natural pain management solutions.
